About the role
We are seeking a motivated and analytical EL1 Assistant Director, Strategic Pricing to lead economic research and analysis that supports evidence-based decision-making on complex pricing and policy issues.
In this role, you will play a key part in shaping pricing frameworks and influencing the sustainability of a major national scheme, contributing to outcomes that directly impact participants and the broader care economy.
 
Key responsibilities
As part of the Strategic Pricing Advisory team, you will:

Lead and contribute to economic analysis and policy development to address challenges within the care economy and human services markets
Apply statistical and economic modelling techniques to large datasets to better understand market behaviour
Develop and refine pricing and policy frameworks informed by robust evidence and stakeholder input
Engage in both theoretical and practical policy considerations relating to current and future support markets
Collaborate across teams, departments, and with external experts and academics to build shared understanding and solutions
Support major policy decisions influencing Scheme resource allocation.
Contribute to stakeholder consultations to test policy ideas and address information gaps
Essential criteria

Demonstrated strategic thinking in solving quantitative and qualitative problems, including development of pricing policies and analytical frameworks
Superior analytical and problem-solving skills, particularly in addressing complex pricing and economic issues and delivering practical solutions
Strong quantitative mindset with technical capability to analyse data and communicate complex information to diverse audiences
Proven ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with experience building and maintaining relationships across technical and non-technical audiences
Energy, enthusiasm and motivation to improve outcomes in the care economy
Desirable criteria

A bachelor’s degree or higher in economics, mathematics, statistics, public policy or a related field, or equivalent professional experience
Experience working on economic challenges within the care economy
Eligibility requirements
To be considered for this role, you must:

Be an Australian citizen
Be able to obtain and maintain an Australian Government security clearance
Complete an Employment Suitability/Police Check
Obtain an NDIS Worker Screening Check (if required for the role)

About Us

Praemium was established in 2001 to simplify and streamline the complexities of wealth management. Today, we offer a fully integrated platform that enables every part of an investor’s wealth to be managed in one place.

Managing over $67 billion in funds under administration for leading institutions, private wealth firms and financial advisers, we deliver innovative technology and exceptional service to our clients.

We are committed to empowering better business decisions through data, insights and technology. Join us as we continue to redefine wealth management in Australia.

 

The Role

We are seeking a Corporate Reporting Analyst to join our Corporate Operations team.

This role is responsible for designing, building and continuously improving corporate dashboards and reporting that drive business decision-making and operational transparency across the organisation.

With a strong focus on Power BI and Microsoft Fabric, you will translate business questions into clear, trusted and well-governed reporting solutions.

Beyond building dashboards, you will play a key role in uplifting data literacy across the business — helping stakeholders interpret insights, build confidence in data, and move towards self-service analytics.

This is a highly collaborative role suited to someone who enjoys combining technical capability with stakeholder engagement and commercial thinking.

 

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain Power BI dashboards supporting executive, operational and governance reporting
Build and enhance reporting solutions using Microsoft Fabric, including governed datasets and semantic models
Translate business requirements into clear, accurate and visually compelling dashboards
Partner with stakeholders across Finance, Operations, Risk and Corporate teams to understand reporting needs
Act as a trusted analytics partner, delivering insights and recommendations (not just reports)
Educate stakeholders on interpreting data, metrics and trends to improve data literacy
Develop and maintain supplier and vendor performance dashboards (SLA/KPI reporting)
Analyse supplier performance trends and provide insights for governance and decision-making
Use SQL to query, validate and analyse data across enterprise systems
Investigate data issues and partner with system owners to improve data quality
Support the adoption of self-service analytics and reduce manual reporting

About You

You are a detail-oriented and commercially minded reporting professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You are equally comfortable building dashboards and engaging with stakeholders to influence better decisions.

You will have:

Strong experience in corporate or enterprise reporting environments
Advanced Power BI dashboard development capability
Experience with Microsoft Fabric or modern Microsoft data platforms
At least 3 years of data analytics experience
Working knowledge of SQL for data analysis and validation
Proven ability to translate business questions into actionable insights
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain data to non-technical audiences
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Experience supporting data literacy or analytics enablement initiatives

Qualifications:

Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) – required
Microsoft Certified: Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate (DP-600) – desirable
Exposure to financial services or regulated environments – advantageous

 What We Offer

Flexible hybrid work environment
Competitive salary package
Comprehensive leave benefits, including paid parental leave
Access to financial wellbeing initiatives and novated leasing
Ongoing professional development and certification support
Modern office with end of trip facilities located on Collins Street, Melbourne

 

How to Apply

Please apply with your CV and a cover letter outlining your experience and alignment to the role.

We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome applications from all qualified candidates.

Please note: Applicants must have full, unrestricted working rights in Australia (PR or citizenship).

A police check will be required for successful candidates.

Your new company


Join a large Federal Government department focused on advancing Australia's economic and strategic priorities. This team plays a critical role in mobilising private capital to deliver sustainable development, investment growth, and climate-focused initiatives.



Your new role


As a Financial Analyst/Economist, you will step into a unique role where policy, finance and economic strategy intersect. Working within a high-performing team, you will support the delivery of innovative investment mechanisms designed to mobilise private sector capital and contribute to government priorities.
In this role, you will:

Manage and contribute to finance and investment programs
Engage with private sector investors, financial institutions and government stakeholders
Support policy development across areas including sustainable finance, climate finance and private sector investment
Contribute to investment performance reporting and program delivery
Assist in the design of new financial mechanisms to attract private capital
Represent the team in stakeholder engagement activities, both domestically and internationally


What you'll need to succeed


To thrive as a Financial Analyst / Economist (APS6), you will bring a strong mix of policy capability, analytical thinking and stakeholder engagement skills, along with:

Experience in policy development, program delivery or economic analysis
Exposure to development finance, infrastructure finance, climate finance or impact investing
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including collaboration with government and external partners
Ability to manage risk, analyse complex information and contribute to strategic outcomes
Excellent communication skills and the ability to build consensus
Experience in monitoring, evaluation or program reporting is desirable.
NV2 security clearance preferably or NV1


What you'll get in return


This is an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of policy and finance, contributing to high-impact investment programs. You'll gain exposure to complex policy challenges, innovative financial mechanisms, and diverse stakeholders while progressing your APS career at the APS6 level.

About BankVic:
  
BankVic is the member-owned bank for those who serve our communities — police, emergency, and health workers. We exist to help our members and communities go further in life, by living our values: Together, Honest, and Brave.
  
As a smaller bank, we offer something bigger — personal service, meaningful relationships, and real impact. We focus on individuals, tailoring support to help each person achieve their goals.
  
Our size also means broader opportunities for our people. You’ll gain exposure across the organisation and be part of work that matters.
  
If you're looking for more than just a job, if you want purpose, connection, and the chance to make a difference, BankVic is your smaller bank of bigger opportunity.
  
The opportunity:

As our Digital Experience Lead, you’ll play a key role in owning the development, ongoing optimisation, and management of BankVic’s digital products and assets including our website, app, digital forms, tools and resources. Your work will directly influence how our members discover, engage with, and enjoy our digital ecosystem.
  
Working closely with the Head of Product & Digital Experience, you’ll implement a comprehensive digital strategy and lead the delivery of the digital road map. This role will lead the analysis and delivery of continuous digital feature improvements, championing innovative solutions and emerging technologies such as AI to elevate customer experiences across our digital banking products.
  
  
Responsibilities:

Lead and deliver the digital roadmap across the website, app, and related tools, driving continuous improvement in end‑to‑end member journeys.
Use data, analytics and member insights to optimise digital features, measure performance, and inform decision‑making.
Facilitate human‑centered design and agile delivery to prioritise features that enhance innovation, UX and engagement.
Partner closely with stakeholders (product, technology, vendors, content owners) to plan, deliver and govern digital enhancements.
Manage digital content and asset governance, including audits, SEO optimisation, structured updates and lifecycle management.
Conduct benchmarking, competitor analysis and behavioural analytics to identify opportunities, trends and optimisation recommendations across SEO, SEM, CRO and UX.

Who we are looking for:

Demonstrated experience (at least 3 years’) in banking, insurance, or other highly regulated financial services environments.
Proven experience (3+ years) managing and improving digital experiences across CMS platforms such as WordPress, Adobe Experience Manager and DatoCMS, including website and mobile app optimisation.
Tertiary qualification in product management, information technology, business or a related field.
Excellent stakeholder management and collaboration skills, including experience working with third party providers to deliver digital solutions.
Highly regarded experience across API integration, Artificial Intelligence (AI) products, and digital analytics.
Understanding of User Experience (UX) design, HCD and Agile principles is highly regarded.
Demonstrated ability to create customer‑driven digital experiences by balancing user needs with internal business priorities.
Strong experience with onsite testing and experimentation (A/B, MVT, personalisation), alongside proficiency in analytics and visualisation tools (e.g., Power BI, Data Studio, Tableau, HotJar).

What we can offer:

Discounts and savings off a range of our products and services.
Access to additional five days of leave.
Ambulance Victoria Membership.
Employee assistance program.
2 days paid volunteer leave.
Generous parental leave provisions.
Ongoing professional development with access to study leave.
Reward and recognition program.

Ad Text:
Our client is a large values-focused Federal Government Agency seeking 2x Pricing Policy Leads (Economics) to deliver economic research and analysis that supports evidence-based pricing and market policy decisions in the care economy.

This role will suit candidates who enjoy applied economics, working with large datasets, and building practical pricing/policy frameworks in complex human services markets.

ROLE DETAILS:
Location (VIC only): Richmond or Geelong
Contract: ASAP start to 31 December 2026 (likely extensions)
Rate: EL1 Level: $602.55 per day + Super
Eligibility: Australian Citizenship required (Federal Government)
Close: Monday 25th May

ABOUT THE ROLE:
You will lead work delivering economic research, statistical and economic modelling, and analysis of market functioning to inform pricing settings and policy decisions. You'll develop pricing frameworks, engage stakeholders to test solutions, and collaborate across teams (including with experts and academics) to build shared understanding and deliver outcomes.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Lead economic research and analysis to inform strategic pricing and market policy settings in complex human services markets.
Apply statistical and economic modelling techniques to large datasets to understand market functioning, provider behaviour and emerging risks/opportunities.
Develop and refine pricing and policy frameworks, including identifying information gaps, testing assumptions and recommending practical interventions.
Lead stakeholder consultation to validate findings, gather evidence, and support implementation of new pricing approaches or reforms.
Translate complex economic and technical analysis into clear advice, insights and recommendations for senior stakeholders and decision-makers.
Work across teams and departments (and with external experts/academics where required) to build shared understanding of priorities and deliver coordinated outcomes.

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Strategic thinking across quantitative and qualitative problems, including pricing policy and analytical framework development
Strong analytical/problem-solving skills with a quantitative mindset and ability to communicate technical insights to diverse audiences
Strong stakeholder relationship skills (technical and non-technical) and ability to work independently and in a team
Desirable: degree (or higher) in economics/mathematics/statistics/public policy or similar, and/or experience in care economy challenges

APPLY:
Submit your resume, or for further information please contact

The Client 
Our client is an Australian Government department responsible for shaping Australia's health systems. They develop policies, fund services, manage regulations (e.g., Medicare, Therapeutic Goods Administration), and provide community information on health and wellbeing.

Role 
As a Business Analyst you will work as part of a multi-disciplinary scrum team to define requirements and deliver solutions to business systems. You will be responsible for populating the product backlog via in-depth investigation and gathering of technical and non-technical information. You will work closely with stakeholders to understand business processes and use this understanding to determine business requirements for systems. You will use a range of analysis techniques to document detailed business requirements and evaluate options for solution. Facilitate workshops with business stakeholders and the development team to ensure a common understanding of requirements. You will beexpected to use a range of analysis techniques such as user journeys, use cases, user stories with acceptance criteria, process mapping, process flow diagrams. As the Business Analyst you will facilitate discussions between technical and non-technical stakeholders and develop quality documentation (including requirements, epics, features and product backlog items, reports, management plans, procedure documentation, testing plans etc.).
 
Responsibilities

Collect and analyse information from multiple sources such as solution design documents, business requirements, program, policy and legislation, IT Strategy documents etc and represent them in a consolidated document as product backlog items or requirements.
Perform the GAP analysis of the current systems and present them in a Visio Diagram covering Products, Channels, Stakeholders, System and Data Flow.
Help build a product roadmap along with the SMEs, Product Owners and Architects and maintain the roadmap as new requirements arise etc.
Document Detailed Business Requirements for Epics and Features using a range of analysis techniques such as user journeys, use cases, process mapping, process flow diagrams.
Provide objective advice, expertise and specialist knowledge to create options analysis and plans for business improvement activities.
Facilitate workshops as required with business stakeholders and the development team to ensure common understanding of requirements.
Work closely with solution architects, software developers, systems analysts and test analysts to provide an understanding of business requirements and evaluate options for solution.
Build and maintain effective working relationships with technical and non-technical team members/stakeholders using advanced written, verbal and visual techniques to negotiate shared outcomes.
Ensure sufficient backlog items are sprint ready for the next sprint prior to sprint planning. This may involve running targeted Backlog grooming sessions or feature breakdown sessions with the team to determine what PBIs need to be created to fulfil the feature.
Produce and provide documentation to business to demonstrate how the proposed solution will meet the business requirements as documented in the Epics and Features.
Participate in Sprint Retrospective to review how well the Sprint was executed and identify opportunities for improvements to the delivery process.
Demonstrated Business Analyst experience in Health, Regulatory or Compliance programs or D365 CRM, Java, Power Pages systems.
Experience in leading the development and tracking of business requirements for major / complex projects, working with various skill sets and resources to deliver successful outcomes.

The Client 
Our client is an Australian Government department responsible for shaping Australia's health systems. They develop policies, fund services, manage regulations (e.g., Medicare, Therapeutic Goods Administration), and provide community information on health and wellbeing.

Role 
As the Systems Analyst you will act as a bridge between requirements and technical development. You will work closely with product/solution architects, technical leads and development teams to design and document systems to meet the business requirements of major programs, or enhancements/fixes for production issues. 
 
You will be responsible for translating complex business requirements into robust, scalable technical designs. The role has expectations to work closely with stakeholders to ensure that solution designs are fit for purpose and are of high quality and delivered in alignment with project milestones, while providing support and upskilling other System Analysts.
 
As the System Anaylst you will also provide expert-level BAU support and lead the investigation of production issues.
 
Responsibilities

Collaborate with architects and technical leads to contribute to both high-level and detailed system designs.
Facilitate technical design workshops with technical teams to define system specifications, consult with Business stakeholders for clarification if needed.
Develop and support re-usable enterprise patterns in development of solutions, whilst ensuring system designs are aligned with business objectives and requirements.
Work with UX/UI designers, UX researchers to ensure the designs are accessible and comply with Digital Transformation Agency DTAs Digital Service Standards.
Manage stakeholder expectations while providing clear, actionable system requirements and designs to development teams.
Lead investigations associated with complex systems and their integration, performing root-cause analysis for problems reported in live/production environment.
Oversee the transition of new features from project delivery to Business-as-Usual (BAU) support.
Develop and maintain comprehensive system documentation, like System Requirement Specifications, Functional / Technical Design Documentation, Data Model/ERD, Database Schema, and System Interface Specification for Integration points.
Lead and or participate in sprint planning, backlog grooming, and technical feature breakdown sessions to define scope of delivery in line with the priorities
Provide oversight, conduct peer reviews and assist the wider team in delivering quality outcomes in a timely manner. For example, working with test teams to review test suites or defect investigations to support department’s quality assurance practices that demonstrate the traceability of business outcomes.
Experience working in an Agile multi-disciplinary team and with the use of Agile methodologies and tool sets such as (but not limited to) DevOps.
Experience with Systems Analysis and Design of systems built on D365 CRM, Java, Power Pages, Dataverse, Azure DevOps.
Experience with SQL, Teradata and reporting tools like Power BI or API implementations.

The ideal candidate should exhibit excellent interpersonal skills, strong analytical abilities with a proven track record of turning insights into action, organizational skills, creativity, and strong project management experience. Senior stakeholder influencing, and evidence of business transformation and growth results are a must. The individual is expected to exercise judgment in coordinating and implementing projects to completion under tight deadlines and to lead projects and influence others assertively. 

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

Primary responsibility will be to harness consumer and market insights to shape strategies that elevate brand superiority. You will be instrumental in creating brands that are unmissable, ensuring they stand out in the market and drive sustainable growth. 

Lead the charge on continuous tracking and monitoring of Market Performance metrics for Ice Cream brand and liaising with agencies responsible. Additionally, ensure the sharing of key insights with the relevant teams to help steer the course of day-to-day strategy on the brand 

Build strategic, sustainable business advantages for the Ice Cream business in the short and long term by combining deep consumer-centricity with a nuanced understanding of business performance and strategy. 

Build effective and collaborative relationships with varied Business Teams and associated functions working on Ice Cream Brands, including Marketing teams, R&D, and Sales to understand key business objectives and thereby identify and communicate the role and value that market data and consumer research plays in the decision-making process. 

Translate business objectives into clear research briefs for the brand, and communicate actionable insights based on data-based findings and logic to enable informed decisions. 

Develop an understanding of the marketing mix creation process and ensure clarity of key risky assumptions and hypotheses to be tested in research at respective stages. 

Learn and employ a range of qualitative and quantitative research methodologies, choosing the methodology/source which is best suited to answering the business objectives for Ice Cream. 

Ensure rigor in research design, data collection, analysis, and recommendations, collaborating with research agency partners where applicable. 

Collaborate closely with the marketing team to help develop impactful brand assets, create compelling content, and amplify brand messaging across digital and retail platforms in the market.  

Strategic Influence: Collaborate across functions to ensure insights are effectively translated into actionable strategies. Present insights and strategic recommendations to Ice Cream BU Leadership Team to guide corporate strategy and product positioning. Influence long-term brand strategies through deep consumer understanding and market analysis. 

Managing relationships across other CMI teams, agency partners, cross functional teams. 

Ability to collaborate seamlessly & lead across agency partners and internal teams 

 

WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED:   

SKILLS, EXPERIENCES & QUALIFICATIONS:     

Analytically minded and numerically oriented.   

Good understanding of market research with an ability to think strategically – ability to design, develop and land research with impact.  

Strategic thinking and the ability to synthesize diverse data sources into actionable insights.  

Excellent communication and presentation skills. Working effectively with cross functional teams and senior stakeholders.  

Strong leadership potential and a team-oriented approach.  

 

LEADERSHIP:    

You are energized by delivering fantastic results. You are an example to others – both your results and your resilience. You are constantly on the lookout for better ways to do things, engaging and collaborating with others along the way.  

Deliver and drive transformational action within the business through key landing points and influencing senior stakeholders.  

You are a Constant Learner - able to keep pace with the environment and lead it. Being up to date on best-in-class insights across topics of interest and the ability to land a message to the satisfaction of all stakeholders is key.  

Advocates for consumer needs and interprets data smartly to spot opportunities.  

Inspires collaboration, builds inclusive teams, and fosters a culture of learning and performance. 

 WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT: 

A unique mix of global scale & start-up spirit—€8.3bn powerhouse with an agile, entrepreneurial mindset. We’re building a new high growth Ice Cream company from the ground up. ​ 

Career without limits—42 markets, international opportunities, and fast tracked growth. ​ 

A performance-driven culture—Freedom to act, disrupt and grow – your success is measured by impact.​ 

A company that celebrates joy, innovation, and purpose—We create extraordinary careers, just like our ice cream creates extraordinary moments. 




ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY:  

With 19.000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wall’s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerry’s, loved in 76 countries, we are the world’s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry We have been taking pleasure seriously for more than 100 years, serving happiness with every lick or scoop of ice cream for generations.   

The Magnum Ice Cream Company (formerly part of Unilever) is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customers’ businesses. Growing our people’s careers.​ Growth begins with empowerment. So we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments – and having fun doing it.​

Job Title: Head of Markets and Strategic Insights
Ad Text:
Most construction businesses know where the work is today.. What about the future.


This role exists to understand where the market is moving next and turn that insight into clear decisions about where we compete, how we grow market share and how our services evolve to meet changing client demand.

We are a privately owned national construction business entering a significant phase of growth. As we scale, we are seeking a commercially minded strategic leader to bring greater discipline and insight to how we assess markets, prioritise opportunities and shape long-term growth.

This is not a traditional sales or estimating leadership role. It sits at the intersection of market strategy, commercial positioning, growth planning and strategic pursuits, helping define where the business plays, how it competes and how it continues to evolve its offering in market.

Working closely with the CEO and Executive team, this role will ensure growth decisions are commercially sound, operationally achievable and aligned to the experience we promise clients.

Importantly, this is also a build role. We are not a large corporate with mature market intelligence capability or perfect data sitting in dashboards. We are seeking someone comfortable working with imperfect information, identifying patterns, asking the right questions and building practical capability over time.

This role will suit someone who enjoys influencing direction in an environment where progress matters more than process for process’ sake.




What This Role Will Shape

Market and sector strategies that guide where the business focuses its effort, investment and growth

Go-to-market positioning and service evolution aligned to changing client demand and market opportunity

Competitor, client and procurement insights that strengthen commercial decision making

Strategic bid and pursuit approaches that improve opportunity selection and conversion

Pricing and commercial positioning that balance competitiveness, margin and long-term market presence

Practical market intelligence capability that supports more deliberate and evidence-based growth decisions

Greater alignment between market opportunity, operational capability and long-term business strategy




About You

You are commercially astute, strategically curious and highly pragmatic. You are comfortable operating in environments where not every answer exists in a spreadsheet and where influence, adaptability and initiative matter as much as technical expertise.

You may bring experience from construction, infrastructure, property, facilities, asset services, specialist contracting or another project-based industry. Backgrounds across strategy, commercial growth, market expansion, consulting, bid leadership or strategic transformation will be highly regarded, particularly where this has involved shaping go-to-market direction, positioning or growth strategy.

Success in this role will require someone comfortable moving between strategic thinking and practical execution, with the ability to operate effectively without large support teams, heavily layered structures or perfect information.

You do not need to come from a large corporate environment. In fact, this role is likely to suit someone who can introduce strategic discipline without creating unnecessary complexity.

We are looking for someone who is:

Analytical and evidence-based

Influential and collaborative

Adaptable and self-directed

Commercially curious

Able to connect strategy with operational reality




Why This Role Matters

Growth is not simply about winning more work. It is about understanding the market, making deliberate choices and building the capability to compete more effectively over time.

This role will help shape how the business grows, bringing greater clarity, confidence and commercial focus to the decisions that matter most.

If you are someone who enjoys identifying opportunity, shaping direction and building practical growth capability in a fast-moving environment, lets have a confidential conversation.

About the Role
 

This permanent role is ideal for a functional Business Analyst who is comfortable operating with autonomy and navigating evolving priorities. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders to understand how things really work, define practical improvements and support delivery across a range of initiatives. Success in this role comes from being proactive, adaptable and outcome‑focused rather than reliant on rigid frameworks.


Key Responsibilities
Engage stakeholders to uncover business needs and pain points
Analyse current‑state processes and define pragmatic future‑state improvements
Document functional requirements, process flows and acceptance criteria
Support delivery teams by providing clear, business‑led insights
Contribute to testing and validation of business outcomes
About You
Proven experience as a functional or process‑focused Business Analyst
Self‑motivated and comfortable working without constant direction
Able to adapt approach as priorities, scope and context change
Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills
Structured thinker who can work through ambiguity
About the Company
Our Client is a well-established, Brisbane-based organisation with a national footprint. They specialise in delivering critical services within a highly regulated environment and are known for their strong governance, collaborative culture and continued investment in technology and process improvement.

For more information or a confidential discussion, please apply.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer - Finance Data Platform (TM1/EPM)
Ad Text:
Play a key role in supporting the Australian economy.
Discounted food, travel, entertainment, gym membership, staff parking and, + more.
Hybrid working environment.

Company

For 81 years, we've achieved success through our ability to innovate, placing a clear emphasis on how we best serve our customers’ needs. As demand continues to grow, we have expanded rapidly, now delivering a superior range of dry goods, frozen and chilled products, fresh seafood and meat, confectionery, paper products, and cleaning solutions.

In recent years, we’ve seen significant growth and invested in our systems and processes, building a close partnership with Woolworths Group that has continued to deliver high-quality service for our customers and suppliers.

In 2024, Woolworths moved to a 100% interest in the PFD business, bringing PFD exciting opportunities as a strategic part of the Group. Sharing expertise across both teams will ensure our continued success and drive further growth and unlock our collective potential.

About this Position  

We’re seeking a Senior Data Engineer (Financial Systems & Analytics) to own and evolve our finance data platform, supporting critical planning, budgeting, and forecasting capabilities across the business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering and finance, responsible for building robust data pipelines and architecture while partnering closely with finance stakeholders to ensure data is accurate, reliable, and fit for purpose. You will take ownership of our current financial planning environment (including TM1) while playing a key role in shaping and supporting future system enhancements and transformation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Design and maintain scalable data pipelines integrating ERP, Financial Planning Systems (Anaplan, Onestream, Workday, and others), and other business systems
Build and optimise data models to support financial reporting, planning, and forecasting (e.g. P&L, balance sheet)
Manage and enhance the financial data platform (Databricks/Lakehouse), ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability
Support and maintain financial systems (including TM1), ensuring continuity of business-critical processes
Partner with finance stakeholders to translate reporting and planning requirements into data solutions
Contribute to system improvements and future state design, including potential financial planning tool migration
Ensure strong data governance, quality, and security controls across financial datasets

What We’re Looking For

To thrive in this role, you will bring:

Proven experience as a Senior Data Engineer, building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines and data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Spark, SQL)
Solid understanding of financial concepts (e.g. P&L, forecasting, budgeting cycles)
Experience working with financial data or finance systems (e.g. TM1 or similar EPM tools)
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modelling (star/snowflake schemas)
Ability to partner with finance stakeholders and translate business requirements into scalable data solutions
Experience supporting and maintaining production systems in a business-critical environment
Exposure to system implementation or migration projects (highly regarded)

What PFD is Offering the Successful Candidate

We are offering competitive remuneration, superannuation and other staff benefits (such as discounts on staff purchases, discounted accommodation, health insurance and banking).

Culture & Values

Customer Focus: Know your external and internal customers. Gain and preserve their confidence because we can only be successful if our customers are successful.
Respect: Treat people at work and in the community as they would like to be treated. Act safely, responsibly, and ethically. 
Passion: Share the passion of making us a successful and dynamic company. Let’s celebrate successes.
Simplicity: Let’s minimise the complications of doing business so customer services are on time, accurate and reliable. Share ideas to improve our company.
Leadership: All of us can be leaders in our day to day working lives. Bring out the best in yourself and the people around you. Do what you say you are going to do.

How to Apply
If this sounds like your kind of challenge, we’d love to hear from you! Click the apply button and submit your updated resume and a cover letter.

Applications close Monday, 1 June 2026 at 12:00 pm (AEST). Early applications are encouraged, as hiring managers may begin shortlisting candidates before the closing date. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be treated confidentially.

As part of our recruitment process, do you consent to undergoing employment checks? These may include a medical assessment (which may involve drug and alcohol testing), a National Police History Check, a Working with Children/Vulnerable Groups Check, and a Licence Validation/Demerit Point Check.

Please note: We do not accept unsolicited CVs from recruitment agencies or third parties. Any CVs sent directly to Line Managers will not be considered, and we will not be responsible for any associated fees or costs.

PFD Food Services is committed to being an Equal Employment Opportunity employer.

How you'll make an impact
The Senior Business Analyst is an experienced practitioner who plays a critical role in delivering strategic business value by leading the analysis and definition of requirements across TFA Domains. They partner closely with Product Managers, Product Owners, and senior stakeholders to shape features, refine backlogs, and ensure alignment between business goals and technical delivery. Operating as a subject matter leader, the Senior BA facilitates effective cross-team collaboration, mentors other BAs, and contributes to the maturity of business analysis practices in a Domain Delivery environment.

We're looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to play a critical role in delivering strategic, customer‑focused outcomes across our domain delivery teams.

In this role, you'll lead end‑to‑end business analysis for complex, multi‑squad and cross‑domain initiatives, partnering closely with Product Managers, Product Owners and senior stakeholders. You'll bring clarity to complex problems, shape value‑driven backlogs, and help uplift business analysis capability across the organisation.

Your usual day in the office will include:

Lead discovery and requirements analysis for complex initiatives across multiple squads or domains
Decompose epics and features into high‑quality, testable user stories with clear traceability
Partner with senior stakeholders to align delivery outcomes to strategic business objectives
Facilitate workshops, backlog refinement, PI planning preparation and solution validation
Mentor and coach other Business Analysts and champion best‑practice BA standards
Manage cross‑squad dependencies, risks and delivery readiness
Define and track outcomes using OKRs/KPIs to support benefits realisation and continuous improvement

Your attributes

Proven experience as a Senior Business Analyst in complex, data‑centric enterprise environments
Advanced SQL skills (joins, CTEs, window functions, aggregations) to analyse, validate and reconcile data
Strong understanding of enterprise data foundations, including relational & dimensional modelling and RAW, CONFORMED and SEMANTIC layers
Deep knowledge of data flows, structures and end‑to‑end lineage across ingestion, transformation and consumption
Hands‑on experience with data modelling, mapping, integration specs and data migration, including validation against business rules
Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear epics, features and user stories with strong acceptance criteria
Experience applying data quality, governance and PII controls to deliver trusted, compliant outcomes
Strong collaboration with engineers, architects and platform teams, bridging business intent and technical delivery
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, confident challenging assumptions with data evidence
Experience in large or regulated organisations (financial services preferred); SAP exposure highly regarded

Job Title: Senior Analyst Line 1 Risk - Controls Assurance
Ad Text:
About the role

At BOQ Group, we believe great risk management enables better outcomes for our customers, our people and our communities. As our Senior Analyst Assurance, you’ll play a key role in strengthening our control environment by leading independent end‑to‑end reviews across the business, focusing on non-financial risks, including data risk, regulatory compliance and operational risk.

This role sits within Line 1 Risk and works closely with business leaders, Group Risk and Internal Audit to deliver practical, commercial insights that genuinely improve how risk is identified, managed and monitored. You’ll bring curiosity and rigour to your assessments, spotting opportunities to uplift controls while also helping shape the future of assurance at BOQ Group through automation and smarter, more integrated ways of working.

You’ll also have the opportunity to influence senior stakeholders, contribute to risk governance forums, support regulatory readiness, and help embed a proactive risk culture across the organisation. This is a role for someone who enjoys joining the dots, asking thoughtful questions and translating complexity into clear, actionable recommendations that support strong decision‑making.

About you

You’re someone who brings a calm, confident approach to risk and assurance, with a solid background in audit or risk management, ideally within financial services or another complex, regulated environment. You enjoy digging into how things really work, identifying root causes and sharing insights in a way that’s clear, balanced and constructive.

You’re technically strong and comfortable working across Microsoft Office tools, with a good understanding of non‑financial risk and regulatory expectations. You’re equally confident engaging with senior stakeholders as you are working through detailed analysis, and you pride yourself on your attention to detail, sound judgement and structured approach to problem‑solving.

You value collaboration and know that the best risk outcomes are achieved by working together across the three lines of defence. You’re also open to new ways of working and are interested in how automation and innovation can enhance assurance outcomes over time. Whether you bring professional certifications or are working towards them, what matters most is your curiosity, integrity and desire to make a meaningful impact.

If you’re looking for a role where your expertise will be valued, your voice will be heard and your work will genuinely help shape a stronger, more resilient organisation, we’d love to hear from you.

About us

BOQ Group is a truly unique group of challenger brands with a purpose – to provide a genuine alternative to Australian financial services for customers and employees.

Our family of brands (Bank of Queensland (BOQ), ME Bank, Virgin Money Australia, BOQ Specialist), each come with their own defined customer audience and brand personality, but share one goal – to build social capital through banking. That’s creating trust with customers, exploring staff potential and believing that tiny gestures and big ideas can change the world for communities.

BOQ Group’s inclusive vision is to foster a workspace that values and leverages difference and creates new possibilities, a place where our people feel confident to bring their best selves to work every day. We embrace difference and believe that our workforce should reflect the customers and communities that we serve.

In 2023, we successfully implemented an innovative Reconciliation Plan, driving positive change and promoting inclusivity across BOQ Group.

Our perks

At BOQ Group, we are passionate about creating an exceptional work environment that nurtures both your professional and personal growth.

Our comprehensive benefits package is designed to enhance your well-being, provide financial security, and foster a sense of community and inclusion. Here are just some of the fantastic perks you can enjoy as part of our team:

Flexible working arrangement - Balance your work and personal life with ease.

Discounted financial products - Enjoy exclusive rates on our financial services across the Group.

Salary sacrificing options - Maximise your take-home pay with our salary packaging options.

Paid parental leave with no minimum requirements - Spend quality time with your new family member without any worries.

Purchase additional annual leave - Need more time off? You can buy extra leave days.

Discounted private health insurance plan - Take care of your health with our special rates.

Employee Assistance Program (EAP) - Access confidential support for personal and professional challenges for you and your family

Access to employee network groups - Connect with peers through our First Nations Reconciliation, LGBTQIA+, Multicultural, Gender, Early Careers, and accessibility groups.

Pride in Diversity membership: We are proud members of Pride in Diversity and actively participate in the Australian Workplace Equality Index.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels valued and supported. Join us and be part of a company that truly cares about its people and community!

How to apply

To apply for this role, follow the links.

BOQ Group is committed to diversity and an inclusive workforce. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People are encouraged to apply.

We celebrate, value and include people of all backgrounds, gender and expressions, sexualities, cultures, bodies and abilities. When you apply, you are welcome to tell us the pronouns you use and please let us know any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

If you are successful for a role with BOQ Group, it is a condition of employment that background screening is completed to ensure that the Bank complies with its obligations under the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter Terrorism Financing Act. These checks may include, but are not limited to identity, verification, reference checks, criminal background checks, immigration checks, verification of academic qualifications, bankruptcy checks.

Please no agency applications.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to work on one of our key government client sites in Darwin, with an immediate start.

This is an initial 6-month contract with high chance for extension for the right candidate, located in the Darwin City.

Scope of Works:

The Senior Business Analyst(s) will be responsible for a full range of business analysis tasks including requirements planning and management; requirements elicitation, analysis and documentation; and capturing existing business processes and proposed changes. The Senior Business Analyst will be required to support the relevant Project Manager or Director in the execution of the project approach and associated plan working with the business, project team members and the vendor/s as required.

Key Duties and Responsibilities:

·       Facilitate workshops, interviews, and focus groups with stakeholders to elicit, analyse and document business, functional and non-functional requirements, and management of requirements traceability.

·       Conduct walkthroughs to validate and obtain approval of business analysis outputs.

About the Role: We are looking for a technically sharp Pricing Data Analyst (Retail) to own pricing data integrity, drive automation, and deliver commercial analytics across our procurement and merchandising functions.

Reporting to the Group Procurement Manager, you will sit at the centre of our commercial and operational engine.  You will own the accuracy of supplier pricing, product costs and customer pricing structures within our ERP, POS and related systems, build Python and SQL automation that replaces manual processes and deliver the analytical capability that gives Procurement, Product Management and Finance the visibility they need to protect margin and grow profitably.

This role combines the rigour of a data governance function with the commercial instincts of a pricing analyst and the technical capability of someone who writes SQL and Python to solve problems, not describe them.  If you are as comfortable writing a data pipeline as you are presenting margin insights to a leadership forum, this role is for you.

In this role, you will

Own end-to-end supplier price list intake, validation and upload into the ERP, POS and other systems, eliminating manual spreadsheet workarounds

Operate as a power user of Pronto Xi across pricing, procurement and inventory, understanding how data flows end-to-end

Build SOPs and data governance documentation that reduces key-person dependency across the Procurement function

Partner with IT, Product Management and Finance on ERP improvement initiatives and analytics capability uplift

Required Skills and Experience

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics or Data Science is required

Retail experience is required, and Ag retail is highly regarded

5yrs Intermediate Python (pandas, numpy or equivalent) for automation scripts and data pipelines

2yrs Intermediate SQL, where you write queries to extract, transform and analyse data as a matter of course

Strong analytical capability across pricing, margin analysis, category performance or similar merchandising analytics

Advanced Excel skills for building and maintaining complex reports

ERP experience across pricing, procurement or inventory modules (eg. Pronto Xi, SAP, Dynamics or Oracle)

Ability to translate complex analytical outputs into plain commercial language for non-technical stakeholders

Bonus points for:  Power BI dashboard development, Pronto Xi, Python automation libraries (pandas, openpyxl, SQLAlchemy), or exposure to demand planning systems

Undertake gas and electricity market modelling and analysis.

Engage effectively with internal stakeholders to ensure work is discussed and coordinated across teams.

Communicating analytical insights to a broad audience, including senior management and external stakeholders.

Provide support to team members and other teams across AEMO, discuss, critique and QA modelling work and analysis.

The team is highly collaborative, motivated and known for delivering impactful work in a supportive environment. You’ll join a respected people leader and work alongside experienced analysts and market specialists delivering complex market analysis and modelling outcomes across the energy sector. Strong communication skills are essential, with the ability to interpret technical findings and tailor insights for a range of audiences and stakeholders.

Experience working in analytical teams to deliver quality outcomes on time.

Knowledge or experience in the regulatory and investment framework for energy markets.

Experience writing technical reports and other documents that convey modelling outcomes and complex analysis in simple terms.

Company Description

Fairness feels good

Make a real impact at AFCA. Where fairness drives every decision. Help us deliver world-class, independent complaints resolution for Australians. As a not-for-profit and progressive financial ombudsman, we’re championing positive change. Achieving our purpose takes progressive thinking, accountability and resilience. At AFCA, our inclusive leadership values every voice. We offer our people flexible work options, thoughtful benefits and opportunities to deepen expertise. Flourish in a diverse, caring culture. Feel the difference of belonging to an organisation intentionally designed to put people first.

Job Description

As we expand our use of data and artificial intelligence, we’re committed to doing it safely, ethically and thoughtfully, always with fairness at the centre.

In this newly created position, The Senior Manager - Data & AI Governance, will lead AFCA’s approach to responsible data and AI adoption. This role owns the end‑to‑end Data & AI governance lifecycle, from frameworks and policy design through to evaluation, assurance and ongoing oversight, ensuring data and AI initiatives deliver value while managing risk.

Reporting to the Head of Data & Analytics, you’ll partner closely with leaders across the organisation to embed strong governance practices that support innovation while protecting our people, customers and community.

What you’ll do

Own and maintain AFCA’s Data and AI governance frameworks, aligned to regulatory developments, Australia’s AI Ethics Principles and organisational needs
Lead data quality standards and practices, ensuring data is fit for purpose for operational use and AI applications
Support the evaluation and assessment of AI tools, including testing for accuracy, bias, reliability and fitness for purpose
Integrate data and AI governance with privacy, information management and information security frameworks, from approval through to implementation
Drive cross‑functional governance forums, coordinating assessments and preparing clear recommendations for decision‑making bodies
Maintain enterprise inventories of approved data and AI tools and key data assets, leading periodic suitability and compliance reviews
Monitor external developments in AI regulation, ethics and assurance, advising leaders on implications and required actions
Lead, coach and develop the Manager, Data Governance and Information Management and their team, ensuring alignment across governance activities
Qualifications

To be successful in this role, you will need:

Extensive experience in governance, risk, compliance or technology roles, with a focus on AI governance, data governance or AI assurance.
Demonstrated experience designing and implementing governance frameworks for AI or emerging technology in a regulated or compliance-sensitive environment.
Experience leading or participating in cross-functional working groups or governance committees.
Experience in financial services, digital‑first organisations or highly regulated environments highly regarded.
Strong hands‑on delivery leadership, with the ability to move between strategic oversight and execution.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders and complex stakeholder groups.
Strong commercial, risk and dependency management capability.
Clear communicator able to simplify complexity and build trust across business and technology domains.
Growth mindset with a practical, outcome‑focused approach.
Resilient, adaptable and calm in high‑change environments.
Additional Information
Silver AWEI Accreditation 2025 – Recognised for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion.
Accredited Family Friendly Workplace – Supporting work-life balance and inclusivity.
Hybrid working – Flexible arrangements with two days a week in our modern offices designed for collaboration and wellbeing.
Additional and inclusive leave options – Flexible public holidays, gender affirmation leave, women’s health leave, and bonus paid time off over the end of year holiday period.

To apply

If you’re passionate about fairness and believe your skills align with this role, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every single criterion.

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. If you require any accessibility support during the recruitment process, please reach out to our team at Car••••@afca.org.au.

We believe fairness starts with people. That’s why we don’t use AI or automated tools to screen candidates. As a result, our processes may take a little longer, and we thank you for your patience.

About AFCA

The Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) was established in 2018 as a private not-for-profit ombudsman service providing free, fair and independent help with financial disputes. The original team has grown to over 1600 dedicated professionals. Since 2018, AFCA has received more than 634,000 complaints, helping to secure $2.1 billion in compensation for consumers. 

AFCA is a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we are committed to responding to every applicant.

About Waste Services Group (WSG)

Waste Services Group (‘WSG’) is one of Australia’s largest integrated providers of managed services, collections, specialised services and post-collections services to the SME resource recovery market.

We connect our customers with tailored solutions they can rely on. Taking the challenge of making every day more sustainable.

About the role

Join us on our ESG journey. Waste Services Group (WSG) is looking for a talented person to join its ESG team to support its sustainability agenda. We are looking for a data-driven professional who is passionate about sustainability and resource recovery, to deliver sustainability data and analysis, support sustainability reporting, as well as support delivery of sustainability projects and communications.

The ESG Data Manager will develop and implement strategies and initiatives to support the data management and reporting of various sustainability performance information across WSG. The role will have responsibility for activities to establish data governance, and ensure that data is accurate, timely and complete, to underpin GHG emissions and other ESG reporting. The role will support project coordination and have an opportunity to lead or contribute into sustainability initiatives. 4-6 years of work experience, preferably in a similar role

Experience preparing sustainability reporting

Experience managing sustainability data and data structures

Strong attention-to-detail

Strong reconciliation skills

Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills

We live our values, all day, every day.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer with 5+ years of experience to join our data team. Our data platform powers analytics for both internal business users and external customers, and plays a key role in enabling data-driven decision making across the organization.

In this role, you will primarily focus on maintaining and enhancing our existing data platform, ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability. You will also work closely with internal stakeholders to design and implement new data models in our Enterprise Data Warehouse as new reporting and analytics requirements emerge.

The platform currently supports the data and analytics needs across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo, and provides reporting capabilities to both operational teams and customers.

Our modern data stack includes Google BigQuery, Fivetran, dbt, Metabase & GoodData. This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with mature data platforms, improving existing pipelines, and delivering high-quality data models that directly support business outcomes.

This is a one year fixed contract hybrid position based in Sydney, Australia. Candidates must have Australian permanent residency or citizenship to be considered.


What you will do

Maintain and support the enterprise data platform and existing data pipelines.

Ensure reliable data ingestion, transformation, and reporting across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo.

Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve issues across the data stack to ensure data accuracy and availability.

Work closely with internal business users to understand their reporting and analytics requirements.

Design and implement new data models in BigQuery using dbt based on business needs.

Maintain and manage data ingestion pipelines.

Deliver clean, well-structured datasets that power reporting and dashboards in GoodData & Google sheets.

Optimize BigQuery queries and data models for performance and cost efficiency.

Ensure data quality, documentation, and governance across the data platform.

Contribute to improving data engineering standards, monitoring, and platform reliability.


What we are looking for

5+ years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering roles.

Strong experience with SQL and data modeling in modern cloud data warehouses.

Hands-on experience with Google BigQuery.

Experience using dbt for transformation and data modeling.

Experience with data ingestion tools such as Fivetran or similar ELT platforms.

Experience supporting analytics platforms or BI tools.

Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modeling.

Experience maintaining and operating production data pipelines and platforms.

Ability to work with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data models.

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.

Good documentation and communication skills.
